content:
# Revelation Chapter 13 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 286-287 | **Source Image:** REV_013_page_001.jpg, REV_013_page_002.jpg

1. Then I took my stand on the sand at the edge of the sea. And I watched as a beast came rising up out of the ocean -- ten horns, seven heads, a royal crown on each horn, and on every head a blasphemous title.

2. The beast I saw resembled a leopard, but its feet were massive like a bear's and its mouth gaped wide like a lion's. The dragon handed over to this beast his own power, his own throne, and sweeping authority.

3. One of the beast's heads looked as though it had been dealt a fatal blow, yet the death-wound had healed. The whole world stood amazed and followed after the beast.

4. People worshipped the dragon for giving the beast such authority, and they worshipped the beast itself, saying, "Who can compare with the beast? Who could possibly wage war against it?"

5. The beast was given a mouth that boasted and blasphemed, and it was permitted to exercise its power for forty-two months.

6. It opened its mouth to hurl blasphemies against God -- slandering his name, his dwelling place, and all those who have their home in heaven.

7. It was allowed to wage war against God's holy people and to overpower them. And it was given authority over every tribe, every people, every language, and every nation.

8. All who live on the earth will worship the beast -- everyone whose name has not been written since the foundation of the world in the Book of Life belonging to the Lamb who was slaughtered.

9. Whoever has ears must listen carefully:

10. If anyone is bound for captivity, into captivity they will go. If anyone is to be killed by the sword, by the sword they will die. This is the call for God's people to stand firm in endurance and faith.

11. Then I saw a second beast, this one climbing up out of the earth. It had two horns like a lamb's, but when it spoke, it sounded like a dragon.

12. It wielded the full authority of the first beast on its behalf, compelling the earth and everyone on it to worship the first beast -- the one whose fatal wound had been healed.

13. It performed spectacular signs, even making fire fall from the sky to the earth while everyone watched.

14. Through these signs that it was empowered to perform on behalf of the first beast, it deceived the people of the earth, persuading them to construct an image in honor of the beast that had survived the sword-wound and come back to life.

15. The second beast was given the power to breathe life into the image of the first beast, so that the image could actually speak -- and it ordered the death of anyone who refused to worship it.

16. It also forced everyone -- small and great, rich and poor, free and enslaved -- to receive a brand on their right hand or on their forehead.

17. No one could buy or sell anything unless they bore this brand: either the beast's name or the number that represents its name.

18. This calls for wisdom. Let the person who has understanding calculate the beast's number, for it is a human number. Its number is 666.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**13:1 -- "I took my stand":** Sinaiticus reads the first person (estathen, "I stood"), placing John himself on the seashore as the visionary witness. Many later manuscripts shift the subject to the dragon from the previous chapter. The Sinaiticus reading makes the scene more personally grounded -- John positions himself as a watcher at the threshold between land and abyss.

**13:1 -- "a blasphemous title" (plural):** Sinaiticus has the plural "names of blasphemy" on the heads. I render this as "a blasphemous title" on "every head" to convey the distribution naturally in English while preserving the plurality.

**13:6 -- "all those who have their home in heaven":** The Greek appositional phrase ("his tabernacle, those tabernacling in heaven") is grammatically ambiguous. It likely identifies God's dwelling-place with his heavenly people. I unfold the apposition into natural English rather than leaving it syntactically stacked.

**13:10 -- The captivity saying:** This compressed prophetic oracle functions as a call to patient endurance rather than armed resistance. The meaning is: whatever fate God's people face, they must accept it with faith rather than retaliate. I render the final clause to bring out this exhortation clearly.

**13:15 -- "breathe life into the image":** The Greek pneuma ("spirit/breath") is used here -- the false beast parodies divine creation by animating an idol. The dynamic force of this passage is the horror of a counterfeit creation.

**13:18 -- "666":** Sinaiticus clearly reads chi-xi-stigma (666). The number encodes a human identity through ancient number-letter correspondence (gematria). I preserve the call to "calculate" to honor the original's intellectual challenge to the reader.

-- PNEUMA
